Class AbstractIntegerLiteral

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.JavaSourceElement
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.JavaProgramElement
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.expression.Literal
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.expression.literal.AbstractIntegerLiteral
All Implemented Interfaces:
Expression, ModelElement, ProgramElement, SourceElement, TerminalProgramElement, EqualsModProperty<SourceElement>, SyntaxElement, TerminalSyntaxElement
Direct Known Subclasses:
CharLiteral, IntLiteral, LongLiteral
public abstract class AbstractIntegerLiteral extends Literal
This class is a superclass for integer literals (Int, Long, Char). It provides a getValue() method to receive the actual value of the literal as well as getValueString() to get a String representation. Subclasses of this class perform range checks at creation time. This means once a literal is created it is certainly valid.

  • Method Details

    • getValue

      public abstract long getValue()
      Returns:
      the actual value of the literal as a long

    • getValueString

      public abstract String getValueString()
      Returns:
      the actual value of the literal converted to a decimal String. If the literal represents a negative value, the first character is a '-' sign.

      Specified by:

    • representsDecLiteral

      public static boolean representsDecLiteral(String literalStr)
      Checks if the prefix of the given String indicates a decimal literal. This method does not check if the literal is actually valid, it just checks the prefix indicating the base of the literal. The base prefix is found even if the String contains a preceding sign ('+' or '-').
      Parameters:
      literalStr - the given String to check
      Returns:
      true iff the String represents a decimal literal, which means it does neither have a hexadecimal ("0x"), binary ("0b"), nor octal ("0") prefix. Note that the literal "0" is decimal too.
